evals.prompt: How do I set up Prisma in a NestJS application?
evals.expected_output: Create PrismaService extending PrismaClient and implementing OnModuleInit — call await this.$connect() in onModuleInit. Decorate with @Injectable(). Create a @Global() PrismaModule that provides and exports PrismaService. Import PrismaModule in AppModule. Inject PrismaService via constructor in any service that needs database access.

evals.prompt: How do I run multiple Prisma operations atomically so they all succeed or all fail?
evals.expected_output: Use prisma.$transaction(). For independent operations, pass an array: prisma.$transaction([op1, op2]). For operations that depend on each other (conditional logic), use the interactive form: prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => { ... }) — use tx instead of prisma inside the callback. Any thrown error rolls back all operations in the transaction.

evals.prompt: How do I run Prisma migrations in production?
evals.expected_output: Use npx prisma migrate deploy — it applies pending migrations without prompting, safe for CI/CD and production. Never use prisma migrate dev in production — it resets and recreates the database. Run prisma generate after schema changes to regenerate the client. Commit the prisma/migrations/ directory to git — never edit migration files after they are applied.

should_not_trigger: How do I use TypeORM with an Express application?
Set up SQLAlchemy async sessions in a FastAPI service
Configure Prisma with a Next.js Server Actions workflow
